DEFENDANTS QUINN GONZALES AND JAMES GONZALES' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT & JUDGMENT, AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED ANSWER IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 19, 2014, TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY A CHANCE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE OPPOSING PARTY SHALL PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT ONLY RECEIVED COUNSEL AUERBACH'S DECLARATION AND NO OTHER OPPOSITION PAPERS. THE COURT RECEIVED THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS QUINN GONZALES AND JAMES GONZALES' MOTION To Set Aside Default & Judgment, And For Leave To File Amended Answer is continued to February 19, 2014, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall provide a courtesy copy of the opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court only received counsel Auerbach's declaration and no other opposition papers. The Court received the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Dec-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Carl Salceido, #2031.

Ruth Elin Auerbach, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Terisita Gonzales, who was unable to attend today's hearing.
Jaime Antero, plaintiff's brother, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Quinn Gonzales or James Gonzales.
Defaults were entered against defendant on October 14, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, December 10, 2009.
Declaration of Teresita Gonzales in Support of Entry of Default Judgment was filed today, December 10, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Teresita Gonzales, and against the Defaulted Defendants Quinn Gonzales and James Gonzales as follows:

Damages/Principal - $650,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $337,643.84.
Costs - $967.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $988,610.84.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
